top_stories.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
top_stories.summary: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks target Microsoft 365 accounts by stealing authentication tokens through fake prompts and OAuth flows, bypassing MFA in seconds. The attacks exploit OAuth consent mechanisms to hijack sessions without needing user credentials, rendering traditional MFA protections ineffective.

top_stories.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
top_stories.summary: A malware strain called 'Djinn' stealer is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The infostealer specifically targets credentials that connect development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

top_stories.title: Identity Lifecycle Management Wasn't Built for AI Agents
top_stories.summary: The article examines structural gaps in traditional Identity Governance and Administration (IGA) tools when applied to AI agents, which lack the employment records, managers, and departure dates that identity lifecycle management was designed around. The piece covers how autonomous AI principals create governance blind spots in enterprise environments that existing IGA frameworks were not built to address.

top_stories.title: Managing local administrator passwords with Windows LAPS and Microsoft Intune
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Intune has integrated with Windows Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) to automate local administrator account management on enrolled devices. The integration uses the Windows LAPS Configuration Service Provider (CSP) to handle password rotation and local account security for managed endpoints.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Security June 2026: new features and record Patch Tuesday
top_stories.summary: Microsoft's June 2026 security updates introduce new identity backup and recovery capabilities alongside AI-driven vulnerability scanning, endpoint protection for local AI agents, and expanded multicloud coverage. The release is part of Microsoft's broader security portfolio update tied to its June 2026 Patch Tuesday cycle.

news.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "ARToken" has been identified as an affiliate of the EvilTokens phishing platform, targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The toolkit is designed to compromise Microsoft 365 credentials and sessions, posing direct threats to identity and authentication controls in enterprise environments.

news.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
news.summary: Researchers at Jamf Threat Labs discovered a new macOS infostealer called PamStealer that abuses the macOS Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) framework to steal login passwords. The malware is distributed as a fake Maccy clipboard manager app and uses PAM checks to harvest credentials from infected systems.

news.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
news.summary: Ransomware groups linked to the Anubis operation are exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 vulnerability (CVE-2025-5777) to gain initial access to target environments. The attacks involve credential access techniques, supply chain credential theft, and lateral movement using legitimate Remote Management and Monitoring tools.

news.title: ToddyCat-Linked Umbrij Malware Abuses OAuth to Access Gmail via Google API
news.summary: Kaspersky has attributed a new malware called Umbrij to the threat actor ToddyCat, which abuses OAuth to gain unauthorized access to Gmail accounts via the Google API. The malware targets corporate email communications by compromising API-based access rather than traditional credential theft.

news.title: FortiBleed Credential Theft Linked to INC and Lynx Ransomware Operations
news.summary: A credential theft campaign dubbed "FortiBleed" has been attributed to INC and Lynx ransomware operations, involving mass theft of FortiGate credentials for use in follow-on intrusions. An operator linked to FortiBleed's infrastructure was found managing negotiation panels for both ransomware groups, directly connecting the stolen credentials to ransomware deployment.

news.title: 19-Year-Old Scattered Spider Suspect Extradited to Face U.S. Hacking Charges
news.summary: A 19-year-old alleged member of the Scattered Spider hacking group has been extradited from Finland to face U.S. charges of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud. Peter Stokes appeared in a Chicago federal court on June 30, where he was ordered held in custody.

news.title: Microsoft Purview unifies insider risk alerts and user context for faster triage
news.summary: Microsoft has updated Purview Insider Risk Management with a unified alert experience that consolidates classic alerts and agent-generated insights into a single queue. The update aims to streamline security team investigations of internal threats by combining user context with risk alerts for faster triage.

news.title: Teleport Earns Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year
news.summary: Teleport has received the Platinum 2026 Pinnacle Award for Zero Trust Solution of the Year. The award recognizes Teleport's Infrastructure Identity platform for its zero trust architecture, which secures AI agents, humans, machines, and workloads.

news.title: BIO-key Showcases Passwordless & Biometric Identity Solutions at Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th
news.summary: BIO-key will showcase its passwordless and biometric identity solutions at a Jordan Banking Cybersecurity Workshop in Amman on July 6th. The company, which specializes in biometric-powered identity and access management, is participating in the event targeting the banking sector.

news.title: Microsoft Accelerates Post-Quantum Cryptography Shift to 2029
news.summary: Microsoft announced it is accelerating its post-quantum cryptography roadmap with a target of 2029, citing faster-than-expected advances in quantum computing research. Azure CTO Mark Russinovich stated that quantum research developments have shifted the risk horizon, making earlier replacement of existing encryption standards necessary.

news.title: Show HN: Identity Layer for Agents and Autonomous AI
news.summary: A developer has released an open-source identity layer project for AI agents and autonomous systems, published on GitHub under authsec-ai/authsec-ai. The project implements OAuth 2.1, CIBA (Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication), SPIFFE workload identity for agent-to-agent authentication, a secret vault, and MFA.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to address machine and service account identity governance as agentic AI workloads increase demand for NHI management solutions.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and compromise accounts.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, briefly seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for the attack were circulated on Telegram, demonstrating a social engineering technique that bypassed Meta's account authentication controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries from both chambers. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems in a public GitHub repository. The leaked files reportedly detailed CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant government credential exposure incident.
